frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver (dominant hand) approaching first peg from upper right. Grasper tips aligned with peg post. Initial grasp preparation visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good approach angle; instrument trajectory centered on target

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping rubber object on upper center peg. Object secure in grasper jaws.
Right needle driver lifting object upward off peg. Left grasper (non-dominant) positioned passively at rest.
Object held mid-air by right grasper. Object suspended above field, clear of all pegs.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Object held stable; no rotation or instability visible

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver approaching target peg (lower center). Object positioned for placement.
Object placed on peg. Right grasper releasing object; object sits secure on peg post.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean placement; object fully seated on peg

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper (non-dominant hand) approaching newly placed object on lower peg. Right grasper disengaged.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth hand transition; left grasper well-controlled

frame_analyses.description: Left grasper holding object mid-air. Object lifted cleanly; trajectory smooth and controlled.
Left grasper approaching original peg location (upper center area). Object positioning for placement.
Object placed back on original peg by left grasper. Task approaching completion for first object cycle.
Right grasper approaching second rubber object on remaining upper peg. Beginning second transfer cycle.
Multiple objects now distributed across pegs showing progression. Video frames show advancing through subsequent transfers of remaining 5 objects.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Consistent technique maintained across repeated cycles; smooth rhythm established

phase_timings.notes: Initial 6 pickups with dominant hand; includes grasp initiation and lift sequence
Continuous mid-air transport across all 6 objects through bidirectional transfers
Midair transfer by left hand back to original peg set
Final placements returning objects to starting pegs

penalties.description: No dropped objects, objects remaining on pegs at task completion, or placement errors detected

confidence_rationale: Completion time clearly visible from frame sequence. All 6 objects successfully transferred bidirectionally with no drops or placement errors observed. Camera angle provides clear visualization of peg board and object placement throughout. Timing markers implicit in frame progression.
technique_summary: Solid intermediate-to-expert performance. Trainee demonstrated clean bidirectional transfer of all 6 objects with consistent technique throughout. No penalties incurred. Completion time of 89 seconds places performance in upper-intermediate range (expert benchmark 40-60s, intermediate 60-120s). Smooth hand transitions and confident object handling evident.
strengths: All 6 objects successfully transferred without drops
Smooth bidirectional transfer technique with good hand control
Efficient peg identification and accurate placements
Non-dominant hand demonstrated proficiency equivalent to dominant hand
No objects remaining on wrong pegs at completion
improvement_suggestions: Reduce completion time from 89s to sub-60s range through faster grasp-release cycles
Minimize hesitation between object pickups and placements
Practice maintaining consistent speed through all 6 bilateral transfers
